etymology.description: semantic 目(目) (mù, "eye") + phonetic 亡(亡) (wáng) -> sense of being lost or disoriented, particularly in vision -> bewildered
etymology.interpretation: The character combines 目(目) (mù, "eye") as the semantic component with 亡(亡) (wáng) as the phonetic component, suggesting a state where one's vision is metaphorically lost or confused, reflecting the meaning of being bewildered.
